ocr.text: The data presented in the table provides a comprehensive overview of the changes in Forced Vital Capacity (FVC)
measurements across different case classifications. Specifically, the comparison of pre- and post-intervention values highlights
the significant improvements observed, with the average FVC increasing from 1.70 litres to 1.85 litres, yielding a P value of
Parameter  Responders  Nonresponders
Pre  Post  P value  Pre  Post  P value
Prednisolone (mg)  19  10  .02  21  13.5  .95
Case classification  Cases  Crude rate  Cases  Crude rate  Cases  Crude rate  Cases  Crude rate  Cases  Crude rate  Cases
Method  Definition
Cell cross-correlations  Measures the degree to which neurons in two structures fire spikes at the same time
Phase locking  Measures how the firing of neurons in one region is modulated by the phase of LFP oscillations in another region
Coherence  Measures whether the phase relationship of LFP oscillations in two structures is consistent over time
Power correlations  Measures whether fluctuations in the power of LFP oscillations in two structures are correlated over time
Functional connectivity  Measures whether fluctuations in the fMRI BOLD signal in two brain regions are correlated over time
This statistical significance suggests that the interventions implemented may have a meaningful impact on respiratory function.
Furthermore, the table delineates cases based on classification, offering insight into the distribution and crude rates associated
with each category. Notably, in the "Imported" case classification, there were zero recorded cases, indicating a potential area for
further investigation regarding the epidemiology of respiratory conditions in this population. The crude rates across the various
classifications serve to contextualize the prevalence and incidence of FVC-related issues, thereby aiding in the assessment of
public health strategies and resource allocation. Overall, these findings underscore the importance of continuous monitoring and
evaluation in respiratory health interventions.
